repository.name: orion-ui
repository.description: Orion is a retro-styled, cross-platform UI framework written in C. The framework is not embedded or mobile-targeted — it is a desktop GUI framework designed for Linux, macOS, and Windows. Its central design philosophy is to faithfully reproduce the Windows API (Win32) message-based programming model in a portable, hardware-accelerated context.

commit.message:
fix: correct nameof() references in DecisionRequirementHandler (#241)

PR #228 fixed incorrect nameof() usage in RealmAccessRequirementHandler,
ResourceAccessRequirementHandler, and RptRequirementHandler, but missed
the same issue in DecisionRequirementHandler.

The handler was logging and recording metrics using
ParameterizedProtectedResourceRequirement/Handler names instead of its
own DecisionRequirement/Handler names, making logs misleading and metrics
incorrect.

commit.message:
[ci-coach] ci: run existing Gridlock Arena tests in JS and Python jobs (#9)

* ci: run existing Gridlock Arena tests in JS and Python jobs

Both Solutions/JavaScript/The-Gridlock-Arena-of-Mythos/ and
Solutions/Python/ contain self-contained test suites that were never
executed by CI — only syntax-checked. This change:

- Adds 'Run Gridlock Arena Tests' step to build-javascript job:
  runs node The-Gridlock-Arena-of-Mythos.test.js (no npm install needed)
- Adds 'Run Gridlock Arena Tests' step to build-python job:
  runs python test_gridlock_arena.py (no pip install needed)
- Fixes Python validation to use find instead of shell glob for
  consistency with the JS job and to cover future subdirectories

All tests verified passing locally before this commit.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* ci: trigger checks

fix(ViewTransition): enable vertical scrolling on small screens

- Active layer uses position:relative so content defines document height
- Enter/exit layers use position:absolute (top/left/right, no bottom) with
  pointerEvents:none so they animate without affecting layout

- Reset window scroll to top on each view change

feat: add UsingOptions(ILogger) convenience overload on WebApplicationSyringe and HostSyringe

Adds UsingOptions(ILogger) on both WebApplicationSyringe and HostSyringe as a
shorthand for UsingOptions(() => CreateWebApplicationOptions.Default.UsingLogger(logger)).
When callers only need to pass a startup logger and have no other options to set,
they can now write .UsingOptions(bootstrapLogger) directly on the syringe instead
of wrapping in a factory lambda.

commit.message:
fix: normalise 8 posts from dual categories to single category (#783)

Editorial convention (SKILL.md) requires exactly one category per post.
8 posts were using two categories (e.g., both Quality Engineering and
Test Automation). Resolved to primary category per subject matter:

- 6 test-automation posts: kept Test Automation, removed Quality Engineering
- why-most-digital-transformations-fail: kept Software Engineering
- copq-in-software-engineering: kept Quality Engineering

Category distribution now: QE=8, Test Automation=7, Soft Eng=4, Security=2

feat: import hourly statistics for Energy Dashboard

Major refactor to properly support the Energy Dashboard:

- Import hourly statistics via async_add_external_statistics
  (96 x 15-min values aggregated into 24 hourly entries per day)
- Initialize cumulative sum from recorder on startup (replaces
  RestoreEntity approach — no more race conditions)
- Change update interval from 30min to 4h (smart meter transmits
  full day data once per day, frequent polling is unnecessary)
- Add after_dependencies for recorder in manifest
- Simplify sensors: remove RestoreEntity, extra_state_attributes

Energy Dashboard setup: add external statistic
'evn_smartmeter:energy_consumption' as grid consumption source.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

and Program.cs updated accordingly.

101/101 tests passing.

commit.message:
Add FCM data message support

- Install firebase-admin SDK
- Add device_tokens table (fcm_token PK, user_id FK)
- POST /api/device-tokens — register an FCM token for a user
- sendFcmDataMessages(userId, payload) — fans out FCM data messages
  to all registered devices for a user; auto-removes invalid tokens
- Wire FCM into DELETE /api/notifications/:id → { type: 'dismiss', notificationId }
- Wire FCM into POST /api/notifications/:id/actions → { type: 'action', notificationId, actionTaken[, actionResponse] }
- Initialise Firebase Admin via secrets.fcm.serviceAccount (gracefully
  degraded when not configured)
- Clean up device_tokens in pruneInactiveUsers and reset-confirm
- Document FCM service account config in secrets.example.json

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

refactor(frontend): auditoria UI/UX completa — 5 fases

- Fase 1: 4 bugs corrigidos (var CSS alias, API method buscarKpis, toast warning, CMV sort)
- Fase 2: emojis -> Lucide icons (15 estaticos + renderCatalogo/Config/openModal)

- Fase 5: dark mode completo (11 overrides: modal, botoes, badges, scrollbar, sidebar)

commit.message:
[ci-coach] ci: concurrency control, job timeouts, and fix JS glob coverage (#7)

* ci: add concurrency control, timeouts, and fix JS glob coverage

- Add concurrency group to cancel superseded in-progress runs; avoids
  wasted compute minutes when commits are pushed rapidly to the same
  branch or PR
- Add timeout-minutes per job (15 min C#, 5 min JS/Python) to prevent
  hung builds from consuming unbounded runner time
- Fix JavaScript validation glob: replace Solutions/JavaScript/*.js
  shell glob with recursive find so subdirectory files are also checked
  (The-Gridlock-Arena-of-Mythos/ and The-Knowledge-Cartographer-Agent-MCP/
  were previously silently skipped — 4 JS files uncovered)
- Add top-level permissions: contents: read for minimal privilege

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* ci: trigger checks

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: save button only appears after actual generation output

The idle event fires on initial session creation before the generation
prompt is sent, which was consuming _pendingDemoPlan prematurely. Now
checks that currentResponseText has content before showing the save bar.

Also moved stray spec from specs/ to tests/generated/.

commit.message:
feat: add UsingCurrentProcessCliArgs and UsingCurrentProcessArgs convenience methods

Adds zero-argument extension methods that automatically forward the
current process's command line arguments to the web application and host
builder, stripping the executable path at index 0 (which
Environment.GetCommandLineArgs() includes but Main(string[] args) does not).

Without these methods, CreateWebApplicationOptions.Default leaves Args = null
so CLI switches like --urls and --environment are silently ignored.

- UsingCurrentProcessCliArgs() on CreateWebApplicationOptions
- UsingCurrentProcessArgs() on CreateHostOptions
- 10 tests (5 per type) covering: args set, exe path excluded,
  application name preserved, new instance returned, null guard
- docs/advanced-usage.md: new 'Passing Command Line Arguments' section
- MinimalWebApiSourceGen example updated to use UsingCurrentProcessCliArgs()

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Wire all 17 human approval gates into agent workflows

- Add WaitForGateAsync extension method for check+poll pattern
- Inject IGateCheckService into all agents, worker, and orchestrator
- ResearcherAgent: ResearchFindings gate before merge
- ProgramManagerAgent: ResearchCompleteness + PMSpecification gates
- ArchitectAgent: ArchitectureDesign gate before merge
- PrincipalEngineerAgent: EngineeringPlan, TaskAssignment, PRReviewApproval, FinalPRApproval gates
- EngineerAgentBase: PRCodeComplete (3 call sites) + ReworkExhaustion gates
- TestEngineerAgent: SourceBugEscalation, TestResults, TestScreenshots gates
- AgentSquadWorker: ProjectKickoff gate before agent spawn
- AgentSpawnManager: AgentTeamComposition gate per agent
- WorkflowStateMachine: FinalReview sync check (Review→Completion)
- Fix test DI to register IGateCheckService
- All 167 tests pass

commit.message:
Add crash types 27-29: ObjectAnimator, InnerClassReflection, AndroidOnClick

Type 27 - OBJECT_ANIMATOR_PROPERTY_RENAMED:
  ObjectAnimator.ofFloat(view, "propertyName") references a setter/getter
  by string. R8 renames the method → NoSuchMethodError at runtime.
  Detection: source pattern match + DEX/mapping confirmation that setter was renamed.
  Fix: -keepclassmembers with explicit setter/getter signatures.

Type 28 - INNER_CLASS_REFLECTION_RENAMED:
  Class.forName("Outer$Inner") uses $-notation for nested classes.
  R8 renames nested classes → ClassNotFoundException at runtime.
  Detection: InnerClassReflectionAnalyzer + ReflectionClassAnalyzer both detect the pattern.
  Fix: -keep class Outer { *; } + -keep class Outer$Inner { *; }

Type 29 - ANDROID_ONCLICK_METHOD_RENAMED:
  android:onClick="methodName" in layout XML resolves by reflection at runtime.
  R8 renames the handler method → NoSuchMethodException at runtime.
  Detection: AndroidOnClickAnalyzer scans layout XML for onClick attributes,
             cross-references method in source, confirms via mapping/DEX.
  Fix: -keep class ActivityOrFragment { public void handler(android.view.View); }

Changes:
- PostBuildAnalysisTask: XML scanning via resDirs, 3 new analyzer registrations,
  3 post-build confirmation branches
- ProguardProtectPlugin: populate resDirs from variant source sets
- ObjectAnimatorAnalyzer: fixed viewInheritPattern regex for constructor params
- ProguardIssue: 3 new IssueType enum entries
- HtmlReportGenerator: icons + labels for 3 new types
- proguard-rules.pro: fix rules for types 27-29 (commented out = crash mode)
- README: 29 total crash types, new type entries + example fixes

Verified: crash mode → 33 confirmed issues (types 27-29 detected)
          fix mode  → 30 confirmed issues (types 27-29 silenced, no false positives)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: Enable autocompletion for arguments after alias commands

When users type an alias followed by a space (e.g., 'cs '), the completer
now expands the alias and provides completions for the expanded command's
arguments and subcommands.

Changes:
- Added _expand_alias_for_completion() method to resolve aliases to their
  actual command names
- Modified get_completions() to expand aliases before looking up command
  completions
- Users can now type 'cs ' and get completions for 'case show' subcommands

Example workflow:
  1. alias cs='case show'
  2. Type 'cs ' and press Tab
  3. See subcommands: show, create, run, organise, check, add, report, status

This completes the alias autocompletion feature by enabling intelligent
argument and subcommand completion for aliased commands.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: resolve remaining PR review threads

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Removes native C++ dependency that fails on Azure Functions Linux runtime.
sql.js is a pure JavaScript SQLite compiled via Emscripten — works everywhere.

POST /internal/control-plane/tasks/{task_id}/sync-github endpoint in main.py,
and 29 solid tests in tests/test_control_plane_github_sync.py.

All 304 tests pass (275 existing + 29 new)."

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/josejavish1/factory-pilot-app/sessions/9e9d747b-c062-4c40-b536-08e68a18dc57

* fix: propagate GitHubAPIError from _find_pr_for_issue; handle InvalidTransitionError as 422

- Remove try/except GitHubAPIError in _find_pr_for_issue: real API errors
  (rate limit, permissions, network) now propagate instead of being masked
  as None (which caused a false no_pr_yet response).
- Add InvalidTransitionError → HTTP 422 handler in cp_sync_github endpoint
  so race-condition transitions return a clear client error, not 500.
- Update/add 3 tests: test_propagates_api_error, test_github_api_error_in_awaiting_pr_returns_500_not_no_pr_yet, test_invalid_transition_returns_422.
- Full suite: 306 passed."

fetch-depth: 0 ensures full git history is available for stamp step.

commit.message:
fix: disable Chromium context menu in embedded terminal

Right-click is used for paste in terminals. The default WebView2 context
menu (Undo/Copy/Paste/Inspect) was intercepting right-click events.

commit.message:
Android perf: setPixelRatio, rAF-gate pointermove, anchor guard

- scrib_renderer.ts: renderer.setPixelRatio(Math.min(devicePixelRatio, 2))
  Caps the WebGL framebuffer at 2× CSS pixels. On POCO 5 (DPR ≈ 2.75) the
  previous DPR=1 framebuffer was upscaled blurrily by the OS compositor;
  DPR=2 is sharp and roughly 2× cheaper than native resolution.

- main_app.ts: rAF-gate pointermove handler
  The event handler now stashes the latest PointerEvent and schedules one
  requestAnimationFrame. The rAF callback drains the event using
  editor.pointermove() (which already uses getCoalescedEvents() for
  stroke fidelity). At 120 Hz touch rate this reduces main-thread
  pixel-work scheduling from 120×/frame to 1×/frame.

- main_app.ts: _redraw_anchor_canvas early-return when no anchors
  Previously, every pointermove triggered a full getImageData + putImageData
  on the doc-sized anchor canvas even when no anchors existed. Now the
  function returns immediately if anchor_manager.anchors.length === 0.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
